subject: Project Management
title: The Olympic Cost Curse
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Olympic Cost Curse.

hook.text: Every single Olympics since 1960 has gone over budget. Every one. No exceptions.

buildup.text: The average cost overrun for the Olympics is 172%. Host cities consistently promise they'll be different.

discovery.text: Montreal 1976: 720% over. Athens 2004: 49% over. Sochi 2014: 289% over. London 2012: 76% over.

twist.text: Cities still compete fiercely to host — ignoring decades of data showing financial disaster.

climax.text: The Olympic cost curse is the planning fallacy on a national scale — repeated every four years.

punchline.text: Every host city believes they'll be the exception. None ever are.

quiz.question: What is the average cost overrun for Olympic Games since 1960?
